title.en: Noticing the blocker before anyone named it
title.es: Notar el bloqueador antes de que alguien lo nombrara

question.en: Describe a time you noticed a teammate was stuck and stepped in without being asked. How did you notice, and what specifically did you do to help?
question.es: Describe una ocasión en la que notaste que un compañero estaba bloqueado y interviniste sin que te lo pidieran. ¿Cómo lo notaste, y qué específicamente hiciste para ayudar?

must_include: the specific signal the candidate noticed — not a formal blocker declaration, but the early pattern or behavior that indicated the teammate was stuck (e.g., a repeated standup mention, a Slack tone change, a PR sitting unreviewed)
the named teammate who was stuck — without a name, the story cannot demonstrate that this was a real relationship and not a constructed scenario
the specific action the candidate took to unblock — not 'I helped them' but the concrete thing: a contact they reached out to, a piece of work they took on, a dependency they resolved
why this was the candidate's move to make — the reasoning for stepping in rather than waiting: relevant context, network access, capacity, or skill that made them the right person
the outcome for the teammate and the project — specifically whether the unblock worked and what it allowed the team to do
must_avoid: stories where the teammate explicitly asked for help — proactive unblocking requires noticing before the ask; if the teammate had to surface the blocker, this is not the story
generic 'I was always available' framing with no specific instance — availability is not unblocking; the story requires a concrete action in a specific situation
escalating to a manager as the unblocking move — unless the escalation was the only path, this signals reactive rather than proactive behavior
framing the unblock as the candidate's initiative in a way that diminishes the teammate's agency ('I saw they couldn't handle it so I stepped in')
